english: reduced to want. - gyóṅ-po (gyong po) (cf. kyoṅ-po (kyong po)) hard, harsh, rough, rude, impolite, (srab-) (srab-) k'a-gyóṅ-po (kha gyong po) hard-mouthed; gyoṅ-ró (gyong ro) a dried up body, a mummy Sch.; metaph. dgra-gyóṅ (dgra gyong) a hard, cruel, dangerous enemy; k'a-gyoṅ-čé (kha gyong che) very rude, impudent Mil.
to put on, to wear = yón-pa (yon pa); lús-la gyón-pai gos (lus la gyon pai gos) the garment that one wears Dzl.; gyón-rgyu (gyon rgyu) materials for clothing Mil.

father-in-law, gyós-mo (gyos mo) mother-in-law, gyos-sgyúg (gyos sgyug) parents-in-law Dzl., Stg. (In Ld. this word is rather avoided, sounding, as it is pronounced there, much like the obscene rgyó-ba (rgyo ba).)

1. angle; corner Dzl. 72, 13; lap, lappet, extremity, gós-kyi grva (gos kyi grwa) coat-tail Tar. 98, 10 (seldom used). - 2. school, klóg-grva (klog grwa) a reading-school Cs.; sgóm-grva (sgom grwa) a meditating-school Glr. and elsewhere: sṅágs-grva (sngags grwa) a school for mystical theology Cs.; °dúl-grva ('dul grwa) a training-school, seminary; smán-grvá (sman grwa) a medical school; rtsís-grva (rtsis grwa) a school where mathematics are taught; yíg-grva (yig grwa) a writing-school Cs. - 3. a cell Cs. (?) - 4. sometimes for grvá-pa (grwa pa). Comp. grvá-k'aṅ (grwa khang) school-house, school-room; *láb-ḍa-k'aṅ* (*lab ḍa khang*) W. id. - grvá-pa (grwa pa) scholar, disciple, generally; monk, the lowest ecclesiastical grade; grvá-pa byéd-pa (grwa pa byed pa) to become or to be a monk. - grvá-dpon (grwa dpon) school-master Cs. - grva-p'rúg (grwa phrug) school-boy. - grvá-tsáṅ (grwa tshang) the apartments in great monasteries, where the monks belonging to the same theological confession live together. - grva-tsógs (grwa tshogs) convention of monks. - *ḍa-šág* (*ḍa shag*) cell C., W. - grva-sa (grwa sa) monastery, grva-sa čéṅ-po (grwa sa chen po) a great monastery; a school attached to such a one; mtsan-nyid-kyí grvá-sa žig (mtshan nyid kyi grwa sa zhig) a school of the Tsannyidpa sect; dei stón-pa-rnams (dei ston pa rnams) the teachers of such a school Mil.
1. sbst., also gra-p'ád (gra phad) 'a muzzle' Sch.; a net before the window, to prevent passers-by from looking into the room Schr. - 2. vb. to carve in wood.

1. a beard of corn, awn, °bru grá-ma-čaṅ ('bru gra ma can) bearded, awned plants, such as corn etc. (opp. to °bru gáṅ-bu-čaṅ ('bru gang bu can) leguminous plants) S.g.; the bones of fish v. nya (nya). Zam.: a tree or shrub, prob. the Tibetan furze, Caragana versicolor. - 3. a disease of the genitals, perh. venereal boils (condyloma) Med.

sbst. 1. noise, rumour, talk, Cs. - 2. the principal or most distinguished amongst several persons Mil. - II. vb. = °grág-pa ('grag pa), míṅ yaṅ mi grág-par (ming yang mi grag par) so that not even the name is mentioned any more Pth.

vb. 1. to bind Thgy., C., W., e.g. grés-po (gres po) a load, a burden, also grás-pa (gras pa) Thgy.; perh. also °grágs-pa ('grags pa), °grógs-pa ('grogs pa) q.v. - 2. pf. of °grág-pa ('grag pa). - II. sbst. 1. fame, reputation, character by report, grágs-pa ṅán-pa (grags pa ngan pa) ill name, bad repute Pth.; rumour, report, deí grágs-pa čéṅ-po byuṅ (dei grags pa chen po byung) the report of it spread, was circulated; in most cases it signifies good name, renown, snyán-pa daṅ grágs-pas sai steṅ t'ams-čád k'yáb-pa (snyan pa dang grags pas sai steng thams cad khyab pa) Glr. the whole earth was filled with (his) fame and renown; snyan-grágs (snyan grags) id. (Cs.: good tidings); grágs-pa-čaṅ (grags pa can), snyán-grags-čaṅ (snyan grags can) illustrious, renowned; rgyáṅ-nas grágs-pa čé-ba (rgyang nas grags pa che ba) of great renown, of celebrity at a distance, (of less significance when more closely examined); fame, glory, rnyéd-pa daṅ grágs-pa-la čágs-pas (rnyed pa dang grags pa la chags pas) Dzl., greedy of gain and fame; grágs-pa-čéṅ-po (grags pa chen po) is also the name of a goddess = dpal-lhá-mo (dpal lha mo). - grags = grágs-pa (grags pa): grágs-dod-čaṅ (grags 'dod can) desirous of glory Mil.; grágs-čaṅ (grags can) W. (pronounced *rág-čaṅ* (*rag can*)) famous, renowned; beautiful, splendid, glorious; proud, haughty (in this case perh. for drégs-pa-čaṅ (dregs pa can)). - grágs-°dzin-ma (grags 'dzin ma), Ssk. यशोधरा, यशोवति, the second wife of Buddha, acc. to others the second name of his first wife. - 2. cry, outcry, clamour (perh. better
